channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract
item.description: DHS did not provide a reason for cancelling CISA's neurodiversity workforce pilot, but it came amid the Trump administration's anti-DEI push. The post DHS cancels federal neurodiversity workforce contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second say of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google got hit with a cease and desist order in Japan. Here’s why
item.description: Japanese regulators on Tuesday accused U.S. tech giant Google of violating anti-monopoly laws, echoing similar moves in the U.S. and Europe. Google Japan said in a statement that it found the action “regrettable.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: These 8 photos made me believe the Polaroid Flip is the best analog instant camera on the market
item.description: Polaroid's new Flip is the company’s most accomplished and accessible instant camera, which finally delivers the imagery and usability goods at a “yes, I can afford this” price.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: About the Best Places to Work in IT
item.description: Nominations for Computerworld’s 2026 Best Places to Work in IT program are now open! Click here to nominate a company today.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Former PlayStation boss says 'Nintendo is losing their identity' as it focuses more on hardware upgrades than innovation
item.description: Former PlayStation executive Shuhei Yoshida thinks "Nintendo is losing their identity" by focusing more on the Switch 2's hardware instead of innovation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: FDA OKs trial of pig livers as dialysis-like treatment for liver failure
item.description: U.S. researchers are about to test if livers from gene-edited pigs could treat people with sudden liver failure — not with a transplant but temporarily attached outside the body.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Consoles in the US could be hit with a 145% tariff, which means the Nintendo Switch 2 might get even pricier
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 and other game consoles will reportedly be hit by a 145% US tariff on imported goods made in China.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Future of Enterprise Security: Leveraging AI and IoT to Enhance Productivity
item.description: Discover how Singtel's Unified SASE Convergence integrates network and security in one place, ensuring robust and adaptive cybersecurity measures for businesses adopting AI-driven edge solutions and IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The network blueprint to take your modern enterprise global
item.description: Adopting new technologies is synonymous with improving productivity, enhancing decision-making, and driving innovations today. Almost every enterprise integrates some form of AI/ML capabilities within their operations or tapping into the Internet of Things (IoT) to keep operations at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster
item.description: Shadi Azoum, the NAVWAR’s program manager for the SBIR/STTR programs, said about 60% of their entire SBIR and STTR awards take advantage of Catapult. The post NAVWAR using Catapult to find SBIR technologies faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Stocks rally worldwide after Trump eases some of his tariffs on electronics, for now
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he was temporarily exempting smartphones, computers and other electronics from his tariffs after China announced Friday that it was boosting its tariffs on U.S. products to 125% in the latest tit-for-tat increase following Trump’s escalations on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is data fabric? How it offers a unified view of your data
item.description: What is data fabric? Data fabric is a type of architecture that aims to provide unified access to the data stored in various places across your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google reaffirms $75B AI infra investment as cloud providers pursue divergent strategies
item.description: In a move closely watched by enterprise technology leaders, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has reaffirmed Google’s commitment to spending $75 billion this year on AI infrastructure and data centers — weeks after Microsoft reportedly abandoned many of its data center projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
